context: Shipping the Prove nav restructure (wave certificate pages). The Wave 4 page fixture referenced https://content.da.live/somarc/da-cli-0-6-0/media/renderaissance/parthenon-frame-study.jpg — an asset that existed only on the codebus (git repo), never in DA media.
symptom: da --commit content put of the page succeeded with zero warnings. The rendered preview page served — the pipeline could not ingest the missing DA asset and silently rewrote the src. Failure was discovered by a human looking at the page.
recognize: NOT achieved by the CLI. content put validated sections and host-less /media URLs (f010 guards) but never checked that absolute content.da.live media references resolve. The one asset class it claims authority over (its own org/repo media) was unverified.
contain: n/a — the write itself was the intended mutation; the damage was a broken public render committed as success.
recover: da --commit content put /media/renderaissance/parthenon-frame-study.jpg media/renderaissance/parthenon-frame-study.jpg (binary upload lane, image/jpeg, 246.5 KB, one committed envelope) then re-put + re-preview the page. Verified: src rewritten to ./media_1096636e….jpg and optimized asset serves 200.
gap: f010's sibling: put warns on host-less /media URLs but is blind to absolute content.da.live media URLs that reference nonexistent assets in the SAME org/repo it is authenticated against. Proposed: preflight authenticated existence check (HEAD per unique same-project media reference) — warn by default, --strict-media-urls extends to fail.
fix: da-cli: put media preflight extended to absolute same-project references — client sourceExists HEAD probe (404 definitive, transport/auth unknown → never blocks); warn by default on envelope warnings[] + stderr with executable upload recovery; --strict-media-urls fails with one ok:false envelope + per-asset next[]. Locks: content-put-media.test.js (3, failing on old code) + collector units; 625/625.
